quick_skim.title: Multi-Modal Multi-Agent Robotic Cognitive Alignment enabled by Non-Invasive Consumer Brain Computer Interfaces: A Proof of Concept Exploration
quick_skim.abstract: While non-verbal behaviors and expressive movements are essential for natural human-robot interaction, existing methods often overlook a crucial element: the human's internal cognitive state. Frequently, proactive multi-agent systems can interrupt humans at inopportune moments, leading to cognitive overload and decreased task performance. This paper introduces a framework for generating "cognitively aligned" multi-agent interactions, enhancing the ability of robotic systems to contextually defer communications to the user of an agent system during moments of high human mental workload and engagement. We present the design and implementation of a closed-loop architecture that explores the interplay between autonomous task execution and real-time neurophysiological focus. Using a consumer-grade Brain-Computer Interface (BCI), our approach continuously monitors Electroencephalography (EEG) spectral band powers while a human performs an engagement-inducing task. We propose an engagement-driven pipeline where an HTTP-based signaling mechanism places a primary agent's sensory inputs and audio outputs into a holding state upon detecting high engagement. This allows secondary agents to seamlessly process complex, delegated tasks in the background. Once the human's cognitive state returns to a lower cognitive load baseline, the primary agent releases the queued agent message. Our preliminary results demonstrate the feasibility of leveraging real-time signal processing, Large Language Models (LLMs), and physical robotic embodiments to create cognitively-aware, non-intrusive multi-agent systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-modal cognitive alignment for proactive systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向前瞻性系统的多模态认知对齐
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向前瞻性系统的多模态认知对齐
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向前瞻性系统的多模态认知对齐
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a closed-loop framework using non-invasive BCI to align multi-agent robotic actions with human cognitive state, enabling proactive deferral of interruptions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有前瞻性多智能体系统常在不适当时机中断人类任务，导致认知过载。本文提出一种基于非侵入式消费级脑机接口的闭环架构，通过实时监测人类认知负荷与参与度，生成认知对齐的多智能体交互策略，使系统能在高负荷时刻自动延迟发送消息，从而降低干扰。实验验证了该方法在提升人机协作效率与降低认知负担方面的有效性。该工作为推动认知状态感知的前瞻性人机交互范式提供了新路径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有前瞻性多智能体系统常在不适当时机中断人类任务，导致认知过载。本文提出一种基于非侵入式消费级脑机接口的闭环架构，通过实时监测人类认知负荷与参与度，生成认知对齐的多智能体交互策略，使系统能在高负荷时刻自动延迟发送消息，从而降低干扰。实验验证了该方法在提升人机协作效率与降低认知负担方面的有效性。该工作为推动认知状态感知的前瞻性人机交互范式提供了新路径。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 人类意图识别与人机交互（HCI）：基于多模态信号（特别是眼动追踪）的实时意图预测与交互范式研究。probabilistic modeling（概率建模）或 proactive interaction（前瞻性交互）, especially work that connects or combines: multimodal intent prediction eye movement; gaze-based human-computer interaction; eye tracking intention recognition; real-time intent recognition algorithms; real time human intent recognition using eye tracking data; gaze based interaction paradigms for proactive system response; computational models for understanding human goals through eye movements; probabilistic modeling of human gaze for proactive systems.

quick_skim.title: LLM-Mediated Human-AI Interaction in Search and Rescue: Impact of Expertise on Attentional Allocation
quick_skim.abstract: Human-AI teaming (HAT) increasingly involves AI systems that provide real-time, context-aware guidance in complex tasks. While such systems can improve performance, their effectiveness depends on how they shape human cognition and behavior. In particular, AI assistance can introduce cognitive demands and influence attention, planning, and interaction with the task environment, with effects that can vary across levels of expertise. This work investigates these mechanisms in a simulated search and rescue (SAR) environment. We compare human performance under two LLM (Large Language Model)-guided conditions and a no-LLM baseline, and analyze interaction at multiple levels, including task performance, eye-tracking measures, and planning behavior. Eye tracking provides fine-grained insight into attention allocation and interaction with AI guidance, while behavioral measures capture how users structure and adapt their decisions over time. Results indicate that LLM guidance enhanced task efficiency (higher rewards and victims-per-step) but did not increase total victims saved. Eye-tracking data revealed an attention-guidance trade-off, with visual resources shifting to the chat interface alongside increased pupil size variability. Expertise moderated this effect: novices exhibited passive AI reliance, whereas experts maintained a "verification loop" through persistent environmental scanning. These findings suggest that LLM-mediated teaming efficacy depends on the operator's ability to cross-reference AI guidance with ground truth to maintain situational awareness.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Uses eye-tracking to study attentional allocation in LLM-mediated human-AI teaming for search and rescue
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用眼动追踪研究LLM辅助的人机团队中注意分配

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates impact of LLM-guidance on human performance and eye-tracking measures in a simulated SAR task, finding effects vary with expertise.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文在搜救模拟环境中比较了两种LLM引导条件与无LLM基线的人类表现，通过眼动追踪分析注意分配。研究发现AI辅助虽能提升某些行为表现，但也带来认知负荷，且影响因用户专家程度而异。该工作为设计基于注视的前瞻性人机交互系统提供了实证基础，有助于优化智能系统对用户意图的实时响应。

quick_skim.title: Fast Human Attention Prediction for Fixation-guided Active Perception in Autonomous Navigation
quick_skim.abstract: Human visual attention relies on structured scanpaths to efficiently process scenes, yet instilling this behavior into robot autonomy is in its infancy and hindered by the high,computational costs of existing predictive models. To address this, we introduce GazeLNN, a computationally lightweight,scanpath prediction model that leverages Liquid Neural Networks as its recurrent engine and employs MobileNetV3 for feature extraction. Operating auto-regressively, the architecture predicts sequential fixation heatmaps conditioned on the current visual stimulus and fixation history. Despite requiring only 0.61 GFLOPs, GazeLNN achieves state-of-the-art performance on the MIT Low Resolution dataset achieving 0.47 ScanMatch score. It outperforms existing recurrent baselines across diverse evaluation metrics, while reducing computational costs by 99.40% and accelerating inference by up to six times. To investigate the role of human attention modeling in robot autonomy and demonstrate the practical utility of this highly efficient architecture, we integrate GazeLNN into an active camera-robot control policy trained via Reinforcement Learning. This integration enables human-fixation-guided perception during autonomous navigation, validated through successful real-world deployments on an aerial robot.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: predicts human fixation scanpaths for active perception, relevant to intent prediction from eye movements
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 预测人类注视扫描路径用于主动感知，与基于眼动的意图预测相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 预测人类注视扫描路径用于主动感知，与基于眼动的意图预测相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 预测人类注视扫描路径用于主动感知，与基于眼动的意图预测相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes GazeLNN, a lightweight scanpath prediction model using Liquid Neural Networks, achieving SOTA on MIT Low Resolution dataset, enabling real-time gaze prediction for autonomous navigation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对自主导航中主动感知需要高效注视预测的问题，提出GazeLNN模型，采用液体神经网络和MobileNetV3进行特征提取，以自回归方式预测连续注视热图。在MIT Low Resolution数据集上以仅0.61 GFLOPs的计算量达到SOTA性能（ScanMatch 0.47），为机器人自主行为中的注视引导感知提供了轻量级解决方案。该工作直接支持基于眼动轨迹的意图预测建模。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对自主导航中主动感知需要高效注视预测的问题，提出GazeLNN模型，采用液体神经网络和MobileNetV3进行特征提取，以自回归方式预测连续注视热图。在MIT Low Resolution数据集上以仅0.61 GFLOPs的计算量达到SOTA性能（ScanMatch 0.47），为机器人自主行为中的注视引导感知提供了轻量级解决方案。该工作直接支持基于眼动轨迹的意图预测建模。

quick_skim.title: Flexible decisions arise from resource-rational memory sampling
quick_skim.abstract: Flexible decision making depends on retrieving and recombining memories. Yet because this process unfolds covertly, its governing principles remain unknown. Here we use gaze reinstatement to uncover the hidden dynamics and computational logic of memory retrieval during flexible behavior. As people deliberated on a blank screen, they directed their gaze toward the encoding locations of decision-relevant experiences, and these fixations shaped their evolving choice. A task-optimized recurrent neural network captured both their behavior and gaze patterns by learning to balance retrieval costs against expected gains in decision quality. These results demonstrate that flexible decisions emerge from a resource-rational process in which memories are sampled to construct decision variables on the fly.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses gaze reinstatement to model goal-directed memory retrieval
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用眼动重现构建目标导向记忆检索模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用眼动重现构建目标导向记忆检索模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用眼动重现构建目标导向记忆检索模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper uses gaze reinstatement to uncover hidden dynamics of memory retrieval during flexible decisions, showing that decisions emerge from resource-rational memory sampling balanced between retrieval costs and decision quality gains, modeled by a task-optimized RNN.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前灵活决策依赖于记忆检索与重组的过程难以直接观测。本文通过分析决策过程中的眼动重现（gaze reinstatement）来揭示记忆检索的隐藏动态。利用任务优化的循环神经网络模拟被试的行为和眼动模式，发现灵活决策源于资源理性的记忆采样过程，在检索成本与决策质量提升之间权衡。该方法为利用眼动信号理解人类目标和决策意图提供了计算框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前灵活决策依赖于记忆检索与重组的过程难以直接观测。本文通过分析决策过程中的眼动重现（gaze reinstatement）来揭示记忆检索的隐藏动态。利用任务优化的循环神经网络模拟被试的行为和眼动模式，发现灵活决策源于资源理性的记忆采样过程，在检索成本与决策质量提升之间权衡。该方法为利用眼动信号理解人类目标和决策意图提供了计算框架。
